herb_parts.name: root
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens root, also known as celery root, is a medicinal part used in traditional Chinese medicine, valued for its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The root is believed to help reduce swelling and alleviate pain, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis and gout. Apium graveolens root is also used to treat digestive issues, including indigestion and bloating, due to its carminative and anti-spasmodic effects.

herb_parts.name: rhizome
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens rhizome, also known as celery root, is rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps promote digestive health and supports the growth of beneficial gut bacteria. It also contains apigenin, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, the rhizome is a good source of potassium, which can help lower blood pressure and support overall cardiovascular health.

herb_parts.name: leaf
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens leaf, also known as celery leaf, is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaf is rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals like potassium and calcium, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. Traditionally, the leaf has been used to treat digestive problems, reduce fever, and alleviate menstrual cramps.

herb_parts.name: stem
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens stem, also known as celery stalk, is a medicinal part that contains a compound called apigenin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The apigenin in Apium graveolens stem may also have a potential role in reducing the risk of certain diseases such as cancer and neurodegenerative disorders. Additionally, the stem is also a source of other bioactive compounds like coumarins and furanocoumarins that have been found to possess antimicrobial and antispasmodic effects.

herb_parts.name: flower
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens flower is a medicinal part used in traditional herbal medicine, containing volatile oils that possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flower has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and antispasmodic properties. In addition, the flower is also used to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs.

herb_parts.name: seed
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens seed, also known as celery seed, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The seed is said to have carminative effects, helping to relieve digestive issues such as bloating and gas. It is also believed to have diuretic properties, which can aid in reducing swelling and relieving water retention.

herb_parts.name: fruit
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens fruit, also known as celery seed, has been used in traditional medicine for its carminative and anti-inflammatory properties. The fruit is rich in essential oils, including limonene and apiole, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Apium graveolens fruit is often used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, as well as to reduce pain and inflammation.

herb_parts.name: calyx
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens calyx, also known as celery seed, is used in traditional medicine to aid digestion and relieve symptoms of bloating. The calyx contains compounds such as apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory properties and may help to reduce inflammation in the body. In addition, the calyx has been traditionally used to treat various conditions, including rheumatism and menstrual cramps.

herb_parts.name: petiole
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens petiole is known to contain volatile oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The petiole of the plant is also rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, contributing to its potential health benefits. Its bioactive compounds have been studied for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a subject of interest in natural medicine research.

herb_parts.name: pedicel
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens pedicel, the stem that connects the individual seed to the umbel, is a medicinal part that has been used in traditional medicine for its purported anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The pedicel is believed to contain volatile oils, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal effects. Research on the pedicel of Apium graveolens suggests that it may have potential in the development of new therapeutic agents for various diseases.

herb_parts.name: style
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens style is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the volatile oils present in its leafy greens. These oils, rich in compounds like limonene and beta-pinene, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The style's extracts have been traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues and to soothe skin irritations.

herb_parts.name: stamen
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens stamen, also known as the parsley stamen, has been found to have antioxidant properties and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The stamen contains a compound called apiol, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. Research has also suggested that the stamen may have neuroprotective properties, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as Alzheimer's disease.

herb_parts.name: gland
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens gland, also known as the celery seed gland, contains essential oils with medicinal properties, including apium gravanone, which has been studied for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The gland is also rich in polyacetylenes, a type of compound that has been found to have antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. Research suggests that extracts from the Apium graveolens gland may have therapeutic applications in treating various health conditions, including digestive issues and infections.

herb_parts.name: pith
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens pith, also known as celery seed, has been used in traditional medicine for its carminative and anti-inflammatory properties. The pith is rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and joint pain. Additionally, the pith's diuretic properties make it useful in treating water retention and bloating.

herb_parts.name: midrib
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens midrib is known for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its potential in treating various health conditions. herb_parts.name: vein
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens vein, also known as the celery stalk, contains a high concentration of apigenin and luteolin, which are known to have anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids present in the vein of Apium graveolens have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-cancer effects. Additionally, the vein of this plant is also rich in vitamin K, which plays a crucial role in blood clotting and bone health.

herb_parts.name: apex
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens apex, which refers to the seed or fruit of the celery plant, is often used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and flatulence. The seeds contain compounds like apium graveolens extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties and can be used to reduce pain and swelling. This medicinal part of the plant is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-bacterial effects.

herb_parts.name: base
herb_parts.desc: Apium graveolens base, specifically the rhizome and roots, are medicinal parts that contain essential oils and have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The rhizome of Apium graveolens is also known to have a sweet and slightly bitter taste, which is often used as a flavoring agent in various culinary preparations. The roots of Apium graveolens base have been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.

plant_parts.name: root
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens root, also known as celery root or celeriac, contains a range of medicinal compounds, including sesquiterpenes and furanocoumarins. These compounds have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The root has also been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and as a diuretic to help reduce fluid retention.

plant_parts.name: leaves
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating various health conditions. The leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and indigestion.

plant_parts.name: seeds
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens seeds, commonly known as dill seeds, possess carminative and antispasmodic properties, which help to alleviate digestive issues such as bloating and gas. The seeds also exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, contributing to their potential in protecting against cell damage and reducing inflammation. plant_parts.name: flowers
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens flowers contain coumarin and umbelliferone, which have been traditionally used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in the flowers also exhibit carminative and antispasmodic effects, helping to soothe digestive issues and relieve cramps. plant_parts.name: aerial parts
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens aerial parts, which include leaves, stems, and flowers, are traditionally used in herbal medicine for their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties. These parts are often used to relieve digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation.

plant_parts.name: essential oil
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens essential oil, extracted from the seeds of the plant, is known for its carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular ingredient in aromatherapy and traditional medicine. The essential oil has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, and to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Its antimicrobial properties also make it effective in promoting wound healing and preventing the growth of pathogens.

plant_parts.name: juice
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens juice, also known as celery juice, has been traditionally used to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation. It is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as arthritis and heart disease. The juice may also be used to support the immune system and promote overall health and well-being.

plant_parts.name: fibers
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens fibers are considered a medicinal part of the plant and have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions. These fibers also have antioxidant properties, which help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage.

plant_parts.name: mucilage
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens mucilage is a protective and soothing compound found in celery, which has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and inflammation. This mucilage helps to form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and discomfort. By coating and soothing the lining of the digestive tract, apium graveolens mucilage can aid in the relief of symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome and other gastrointestinal disorders.

plant_parts.name: saponins
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens saponins have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties and are believed to be responsible for the plant's potential wound healing and skin soothing effects. Additionally, the saponins in Apium graveolens have been reported to have antimicrobial and antifungal activities, which can help protect against infections and promote healthy skin. Research suggests that the saponins in this plant may also have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, contributing to its overall potential health benefits.

plant_parts.name: phytochemicals
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens phytochemicals, such as falcarinol, falcarinolide, and sesquiterpenes, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties. These phytochemicals have been isolated from the seeds, leaves, and roots of the plant and have shown potential in preventing cell damage and promoting overall health. Research has also identified other beneficial compounds like limonene and beta-pinene in Apium graveolens, highlighting its value in traditional medicine.

plant_parts.name: glycosides
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens glycosides have been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These glycosides are responsible for the plant's ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, making it a potential natural remedy for wound healing. Additionally, the glycosides in Apium graveolens may also have a protective effect on the liver and kidneys by reducing oxidative stress.

plant_parts.name: sesquiterpenes
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens sesquiterpenes, which are a class of compounds found in the plant, have been studied for their potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These sesquiterpenes have been shown to possess antimicrobial and cytotoxic activities, making them a subject of interest in the development of new medicines. Furthermore, some sesquiterpenes extracted from Apium graveolens have been found to exhibit anti-cancer and neuroprotective properties, highlighting their potential in treating various diseases.

plant_parts.name: flavonoids
plant_parts.desc: Apium graveolens flavonoids have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can contribute to the reduction of oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. These compounds may also have a role in protecting against cell damage and promoting overall health. The presence of flavonoids in Apium graveolens is thought to contribute to the plant's potential medicinal applications, including its use in traditional remedies for various health conditions.
